purpose.value: To function as a digital medical prescription application (Lootify) that manages users (patients, doctors, staff), catalogs medical entities (specialties, medications), and facilitates the creation, issuance, and secure delivery of legally signed medical prescriptions.
purpose.source_quote: Lootify é uma aplicação de prescrição médica digital. Permite o cadastro de usuários, médicos, especialidades, medicamentos e a emissão de prescrições com assinatura digital ICP-Brasil.

context.value: The system operates within a regulated medical domain, requiring strict control over user roles, data integrity (especially prescriptions), and legal compliance for document signing (ICP-Brasil). It manages a lifecycle for users, specialties, medications, and prescriptions, with a focus on auditability and non-destructive data handling (no hard deletes).
context.source_quote: Permite o cadastro de usuários, médicos, especialidades, medicamentos e a emissão de prescrições com assinatura digital ICP-Brasil.

in: Digital Document Generation and Signing (ICP-Brasil PDF)
out: Direct patient-facing interaction outside of the prescribed workflow (e.g., complex patient portal features beyond viewing prescriptions)
scope.source_quote: Sem hard delete... PDF só pode ser gerado para prescrições no estado ISSUED.

capabilities.value: Manage User Identities and Roles (Admin, Attendant, Doctor, Patient) with granular access control.
Manage and govern the catalog of medical Specialties (creation, editing, deactivation by Admin).
Manage and govern the catalog of Medications (creation, editing, deactivation by Admin).
Manage Doctor eligibility by tracking Specialty associations, requiring Admin approval.
Allow Doctors to create, edit, and issue prescriptions for registered patients, respecting state transitions (DRAFT -> ISSUED).
Generate immutable, digitally signed PDF documents for issued prescriptions using ICP-Brasil standards.
Manage Doctor schedules and appointments, linking Attendants to specific Doctors.
Provide secure, time-limited access for patients to download issued prescriptions.

external_interfaces.value: ICP-Brasil Digital Signature Service (for signing PDFs)

external_interfaces.source_quote: O attendant usa o token para criar seu AuthAccount — escolhe entre senha ou OAuth Google.

constraints.value: All core entities must maintain a lifecycle via status/deleted_at, prohibiting hard deletes.
Prescription issuance requires the Doctor to have an active profile AND an approved Specialty association.
Prescription PDF generation is strictly limited to prescriptions in the 'ISSUED' state.
System must enforce role-based access control (RBAC) explicitly for every action.
Data persistence must use PostgreSQL as the source of truth.

success_criteria.value: A Doctor can successfully register, be activated by Admin, and be eligible to prescribe.
An Attendant can successfully register new users (Patients/Staff) and link them to a Doctor's schedule.
A Doctor can generate a legally valid, signed PDF for an issued prescription, accessible via a temporary link.

assumptions: The system's primary interface will be a web-based SaaS application, given the nature of the modules (API, web-saas archetype fit).
The 'Attendant' role's future access to agenda/patients will be implemented via a dedicated linkage mechanism, as described.
The 'snapshot' of clinical content stored upon issuance is sufficient for the PDF generation, meaning no further real-time data retrieval is needed for the final document.
The MVP implementation of ICP-Brasil signing is assumed to be a black-box service integration, not requiring deep cryptographic implementation details.
clarifying_questions: What is the expected volume/scale for the number of Patients and Doctors? (To gauge performance/scalability needs)
What are the specific requirements for the 'Attendant' role's future access to the Doctor's agenda and patients? (Is it read-only, or does it require write access to appointments?)
What is the required retention period for audit logs related to prescription status changes (Draft, Issued, Cancelled)?
Are there specific regulatory requirements (beyond ICP-Brasil) regarding data residency or patient data handling (e.g., HIPAA, LGPD) that must be enforced by the system?
What is the expected latency for prescription issuance (from Doctor action to PDF generation)? (To assess real-time vs. batch needs)

candidate_archetypes.why: It is a multi-tenant, browser-delivered transactional application with server-side business logic, managing user accounts and complex workflows.
It automates structured, role-based business processes (prescribing, managing specialties) over a rich, long-lived data model, fitting the definition of an internal business system.
The architecture is designed modularly (DDD), suggesting it will be composed of several network-exposed services, even if currently deployed as a monolith.

salient_domains.why: Handling sensitive medical data, authentication, and digital signatures (ICP-Brasil) makes security paramount.
Medical records and digital signatures are subject to strict legal and privacy compliance.
The core purpose is defined by its functional capabilities: prescribing, cataloging, and managing roles.
Prescriptions must be accurate and immutable once issued; data integrity is critical.
The system revolves around complex, interconnected entities (User, Prescription, Specialty, Medication).
Specific constraints like 'no hard delete' and 'ICP-Brasil signing' dictate architectural choices.
While the focus is on backend correctness, the system must be usable by Doctors and Attendants to perform their roles.
